Package org.eventb.core.seqprover

Interface IReasonerDesc

All Known Implementing Classes:
ReasonerDesc
public interface IReasonerDesc
Common protocol for accessing reasoners and their properties.

Instances of this interface can be obtained through the reasoner registry ( IReasonerRegistry.getReasonerDesc(String)).

This interface is not intended to be implemented by clients.

Since:
1.0

  • Field Summary

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    static final int
    NO_VERSION
    Constant invalid version, meaning that the reasoner is not versioned.

  • Field Details

    • NO_VERSION

      static final int NO_VERSION
      Constant invalid version, meaning that the reasoner is not versioned.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

  • Method Details

    • getId

      String getId()
      Returns the id of the described reasoner.
      Returns:
      the id of the described reasoner

    • getName

      String getName()
      Returns the name of the reasoner, as written in the extension point.
      Returns:
      the name of the reasoner

    • getInstance

      IReasoner getInstance()
      Returns an instance of the reasoner.

      In case the reasoner has not been registered, or if there is a problem instantiating the reasoner class, a dummy reasoner instance is returned.

      A dummy reasoner cannot be replayed. An attempt to call the apply() method on it will always return an IReasonerFailure. One can know whether the returned instance is a dummy one by calling IReasonerRegistry.isDummyReasoner(IReasoner).

      Returns:
      an instance of the reasoner (might be a dummy one in case of error)

    • getVersion

      int getVersion()
      Returns the actual version of the described reasoner.

      The actual version can be different from the registered version (as returned by the reasoner instance) when the IReasonerDesc was obtained through proof deserialization, and the reasoner version has changed since the proof was serialized.

      Returns:
      a natural integer that is the actual version of the described reasoner, or NO_VERSION if the reasoner is not registered or not versioned
      See Also:
      • hasVersionConflict()

    • getRegisteredVersion

      int getRegisteredVersion()
      Returns the registered version of the described reasoner.

      When the reasoner instance implements IVersionedReasoner, it is equivalent to calling getInstance().getVersion(). If the reasoner is not versioned or not registered, the method returns NO_VERSION.

      Returns:
      the registered version of the reasoner, or NO_VERSION

    • getVersionedId

      String getVersionedId()
      Returns an encoding of both the id and the version of the described reasoner.

      The encoded version is the actual one, as returned by getVersion(). If the reasoner is not versioned, then the bare id is returned.

      The result is intended to be used for reasoner persistence purposes.

      Returns:
      the String encoding of the id with the version

    • hasVersionConflict

      boolean hasVersionConflict()
      Returns whether the described reasoner has an actual version different from the registered version.
      Returns:
      true iff the actual version is different from the registered version.

    • isContextDependent

      boolean isContextDependent()
      Returns whether the described reasoner is context dependent.

      A reasoner is context dependent if its output depends on its external environment: language extensions, additional proof rules, or whatever may make it produce a different output at different times when applied with the same input.

      Context dependent reasoners are never reused directly based on proof dependencies alone. Instead, their output is checked in every proof where they appear, and for all applications inside each proof.

      Returns:
      true iff the reasoner is context dependent
      Since:
      3.0

    • isTrusted

      boolean isTrusted()
      Returns whether the described reasoner is trusted.

      A reasoner is trusted if all of the following hold:

    • it is registered ( IReasonerRegistry.isRegistered(String))
    • it is NOT a dummy reasoner ( IReasonerRegistry.isDummyReasoner(IReasoner))
    • it has NO version conflict (hasVersionConflict()

      • Returns:
      true if the reasoner is trusted, false otherwise
      Since:
      3.1
